surface.name: Dropbox generated access token + cURL files/list_folder
surface.selection_basis: Dropbox OAuth guide recommends a generated access token for quick own-account API testing before implementing OAuth. HTTP docs demonstrate cURL POST to https://api.dropboxapi.com/2/files/list_folder with Authorization Bearer.

alternatives_considered.name: JavaScript SDK Basic example
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: SDK install optional when cURL is documented for list_folder.

alternatives_considered.name: OAuth code flow
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Required for other users; generated token is the documented quick own-account test path.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Dropbox app, generate an access token, and list the root folder with cURL.
assumed_prior_state: No Dropbox app or access token exists.
A terminal with cURL is available.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Authenticated cURL POST to /2/files/list_folder returns folder listing JSON for the authorized account.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: HTTP 200 JSON includes entries (possibly empty) for path "".

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: OAuth guide documents Generate token for own-account testing; HTTP examples use cURL list_folder with Bearer token.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: First API success is an authenticated list_folder response without implementing OAuth or installing an SDK.

prerequisites.requirement: Dropbox account and App Console app.
Generated OAuth 2 access token for own account.

primary_path.action: Create or sign in to Dropbox and open the App Console.
Create an app with the required permissions for files.list_folder.
In OAuth 2 settings, click Generate and store the access token.
primary_path.success_signal: Token value available for Bearer auth.

primary_path.action: POST https://api.dropboxapi.com/2/files/list_folder with Authorization Bearer and JSON {"path": ""}.

excluded_after_success: OAuth code flow for other users
